verses.text: By law a man does not take back a wife who has remarried, yet God invites the unfaithful nation, 'return again to me.' His mercy exceeds the law. God woos back the people who have wronged Him beyond all human pardon.

By law a man does not take back a wife who has remarried, yet God invites the unfaithful nation, 'return again to me.' His mercy exceeds the law. God woos back the people who have wronged Him beyond all human pardon.

By law a man does not take back a wife who has remarried, yet God invites the unfaithful nation, 'return again to me.' His mercy exceeds the law. God woos back the people who have wronged Him beyond all human pardon.

Backsliding Israel committed adultery and was divorced, yet treacherous Judah, seeing it, only feigned return. Her repentance was false. To pretend repentance while keeping the heart far from God is a deeper treachery.

Backsliding Israel committed adultery and was divorced, yet treacherous Judah, seeing it, only feigned return. Her repentance was false. To pretend repentance while keeping the heart far from God is a deeper treachery.
Israel is judged less guilty than false Judah; God calls, 'Return... only acknowledge thine iniquity.' Confession is the one condition. God asks not perfection but honest owning of sin.

'Turn, O backsliding children,' for God is married to them; He will give pastors after His own heart and a glory beyond the ark. Restoration is rich. God promises His returning people faithful shepherds and His own presence.

'Turn, O backsliding children,' for God is married to them; He will give pastors after His own heart and a glory beyond the ark. Restoration is rich. God promises His returning people faithful shepherds and His own presence.

'Turn, O backsliding children,' for God is married to them; He will give pastors after His own heart and a glory beyond the ark. Restoration is rich. God promises His returning people faithful shepherds and His own presence.
Jerusalem shall be called 'the throne of the LORD,' and all nations gathered to it, Judah and Israel reunited. The vision widens. God's restored city becomes a centre for the whole world to seek Him.
Jerusalem shall be called 'the throne of the LORD,' and all nations gathered to it, Judah and Israel reunited. The vision widens. God's restored city becomes a centre for the whole world to seek Him.

God longed to set them among His children and have them call Him 'My father,' but they dealt treacherously like a faithless wife. His fatherly love was spurned. Yet the grief shows how He yearns for their return.

God longed to set them among His children and have them call Him 'My father,' but they dealt treacherously like a faithless wife. His fatherly love was spurned. Yet the grief shows how He yearns for their return.

A weeping is heard on the heights, and God calls, 'Return... and I will heal your backslidings'; they answer, 'Behold, we come unto thee.' Penitence responds to grace. The God who calls the wanderer also heals and welcomes him home.
